title: Dressed electron as a quasiparticle
summary: A quasiparticle can be pictured as an electron moving with a screening and polarization cloud that renormalizes its energy.
assumptions: The charged excitation remains coherent enough to support a dressed-particle picture.

boundary_conditions: dominant spectral weight stays in one peak

applies_to: explaining why self-energy corrections move quasiparticle energies
out_of_scope: proof-level statements about exact spectral functions

promotion.rationale: Manual L2 seed for reusable GW/QSGW excitation memory.

payload.picture: A charged excitation drags a polarization cloud, so its effective energy is shifted relative to the bare reference state.
known_limits: breaks down when the excitation is too incoherent to isolate a dominant pole
